description: Structure, properties, nomenclature, and reactivity of alcohols, ethers, aldehydes, ketones, carboxylic acids, esters, amines, and amides.

problems.problem: What is a functional group and why is it important in organic chemistry?

step_by_step.explanation: A functional group is a specific atom or group of atoms within a molecule that is responsible for characteristic chemical reactions.
Functional groups determine the chemical properties and reactivity of organic compounds; compounds with the same functional group behave similarly.

problems.final_answer: A functional group is a reactive site in a molecule that determines its chemical properties and reactions

problems.problem: Identify the functional group in ethanol ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$) and classify the alcohol as primary, secondary, or tertiary.

step_by_step.explanation: The $-\text{OH}$ group is the hydroxyl functional group → alcohol
The carbon bearing the $-\text{OH}$ is bonded to one other carbon and two hydrogens

problems.problem: Name the alcohol with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}(\text{OH})\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_3$.

step_by_step.explanation: Longest chain containing the $-\text{OH}$ group has $4$ carbons → butane

problems.problem: Draw the structure of 2-methylpropan-1-ol.

step_by_step.explanation: "propan-1-ol" indicates a $3$-carbon chain with $-\text{OH}$ on carbon $1$
Methyl group on carbon $2$

problems.problem: Name the ether with the formula $\text{CH}_3-\text{O}-\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_3$.

step_by_step.explanation: Methyl group ($\text{CH}_3$) and ethyl group ($\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_3$)
Methoxyethane (alkoxyalkane naming)

problems.problem: Draw the structure of dipropyl ether.

step_by_step.explanation: Two propyl groups ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_2-$)

problems.problem: Name the aldehyde with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{CHO}$.

step_by_step.explanation: The carbonyl carbon is at the end of a $4$-carbon chain → butanal

problems.problem: Name the ketone with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{COCH}_2\text{CH}_3$.

step_by_step.explanation: Longest chain containing the carbonyl is $4$ carbons → butanone
Carbonyl carbon is at position $2$

problems.problem: Draw the structure of 3-methylpentanal.

step_by_step.explanation: "pentanal" indicates a $5$-carbon chain with an aldehyde at the end
Methyl group on carbon $3$

problems.problem: Name the carboxylic acid with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{COOH}$.

step_by_step.explanation: Longest chain containing the carboxyl group has $3$ carbons → propanoic acid

problems.problem: Draw the structure of 2-methylbutanoic acid.

step_by_step.explanation: "butanoic acid" indicates a $4$-carbon chain with $-\text{COOH}$ at the end
Methyl group on carbon $2$

problems.problem: Name the ester formed from methanol and butanoic acid.

step_by_step.explanation: From methanol ($\text{CH}_3\text{OH}$) → methyl group
From butanoic acid ($\text{C}_3\text{H}_7\text{COOH}$) → butanoate

problems.problem: Draw the structure of ethyl ethanoate.

step_by_step.explanation: "ethyl" indicates from ethanol ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$)
"ethanoate" indicates from ethanoic acid ($\text{CH}_3\text{COOH}$)

problems.problem: What functional group is present in an amine? Classify $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{NH}_2$ as primary, secondary, or tertiary.

step_by_step.explanation: Amino group ($-\text{NH}_2$, $-\text{NHR}$, or $-\text{NR}_2$)
Nitrogen is bonded to one carbon (ethyl) and two hydrogens

problems.problem: Name the amide with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{CONH}_2$.

step_by_step.explanation: Derived from ethanoic acid ($\text{CH}_3\text{COOH}$)

problems.problem: Identify the functional group in each compound: (a) $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$, (b) $\text{CH}_3\text{COCH}_3$, (c) $\text{CH}_3\text{CHO}$, (d) $\text{CH}_3\text{COOH}$.

step_by_step.explanation: $-\text{OH}$ group → alcohol
$\text{C}=$O group with carbons on both sides → ketone
$\text{C}=$O group at the end with H → aldehyde
$-\text{COOH}$ group → carboxylic acid

problems.problem: Explain why alcohols have higher boiling points than alkanes of similar molar mass.

step_by_step.explanation: Alkanes only have London dispersion forces
Alcohols have hydrogen bonding (due to O-H group) in addition to dispersion forces
Hydrogen bonding is much stronger than dispersion forces alone
Stronger intermolecular forces require more energy to overcome, resulting in higher boiling points

problems.final_answer: Alcohols have hydrogen bonding; alkanes have only dispersion forces; hydrogen bonding is stronger, raising boiling point

problems.problem: Arrange the following in order of increasing boiling point: propane, propanal, propanol, propanoic acid.

step_by_step.explanation: Non-polar, only dispersion forces → lowest boiling point
Polar, dipole-dipole forces (no hydrogen bonding) → higher than propane
Hydrogen bonding → higher than propanal
Strong hydrogen bonding (dimer formation) → highest boiling point

problems.problem: Explain the trend in water solubility for alcohols: methanol (miscible), ethanol (miscible), butanol (slightly soluble), octanol (insoluble).

step_by_step.explanation: Solubility depends on balance between polar $-\text{OH}$ group and non-polar hydrocarbon chain
Methanol and ethanol have small hydrocarbon portions; the $-\text{OH}$ group can hydrogen bond with water, making them miscible
As hydrocarbon chain length increases, the non-polar part dominates, reducing water solubility
Long hydrocarbon chains are non-polar and do not interact favorably with polar water

problems.final_answer: As hydrocarbon chain length increases, non-polar character increases, reducing water solubility

problems.problem: Compare the boiling points of diethyl ether and butan-1-ol (both have formula $\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10}\text{O}$). Explain the difference.

step_by_step.explanation: $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2-\text{O}-\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_3$
$\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$
Diethyl ether has dipole-dipole forces (no hydrogen bonding); butan-1-ol has hydrogen bonding
Hydrogen bonding is stronger, so butan-1-ol has higher boiling point

problems.final_answer: Butan-1-ol has hydrogen bonding (higher boiling point); diethyl ether has only dipole-dipole forces

problems.problem: Identify the functional group in a molecule that has a carbonyl group ($\text{C}=$O) bonded to a hydrogen atom and an alkyl group.

step_by_step.explanation: $\text{C}=$O bonded to H and R (alkyl group)

problems.problem: Draw the structure of N-ethylpropanamide.

step_by_step.explanation: "propanamide" indicates a $3$-carbon chain with $-\text{CONH}_2$
N-ethyl indicates an ethyl group attached to the nitrogen

problems.problem: Name the amine with the formula $\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{NHCH}_2\text{CH}_3$.

step_by_step.explanation: Two ethyl groups attached to nitrogen
N-ethylethanamine

problems.problem: Identify the functional group that is responsible for the characteristic sour taste and reactivity with bases of many organic compounds.

step_by_step.explanation: Sour taste and reaction with bases are characteristic of acids

problems.problem: What product is formed when a primary alcohol is oxidized? What about a secondary alcohol?

step_by_step.explanation: Primary alcohols oxidize first to aldehydes, then further to carboxylic acids
Secondary alcohols oxidize to ketones (no further oxidation)
Tertiary alcohols do not oxidize under normal conditions

problems.final_answer: Primary alcohol → aldehyde → carboxylic acid; Secondary alcohol → ketone; Tertiary alcohol → no reaction

problems.problem: Write the balanced equation for the esterification reaction between ethanoic acid and ethanol. Name the product.

step_by_step.explanation: Ethanoic acid ($\text{CH}_3\text{COOH}$) and ethanol ($\text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2\text{OH}$)
Carboxylic acid + alcohol → ester + water (with acid catalyst)
From ethanol (ethyl) and ethanoic acid (ethanoate)
